all_reviews.reviewText: The Sony WH-1000XM5 headphones offer exceptional sound quality and advanced features. They deliver rich, immersive audio with customizable EQ settings. The superior ANC effectively blocks ambient noise. The lightweight design with soft-fit ear cups ensures comfort. With up to 30 hours of battery life on a single charge, they provide long-lasting use. Connectivity options include Bluetooth 5.2, multipoint, and wired connections. Smart features like "Speak-to-Chat," touch controls, and voice assistant integration enhance the user experience. These headphones are ideal for both audiophiles and casual listeners.

all_reviews.reviewText:
There are a lot of noise canceling headsets on the market but even popular brands are not offering any support for phone calls. They are mostly focused on the person that wear the headsets not the person on the other end of the line. I would not be wrong if I tell you that they are making the things worse for the other person, by amplifying the background noise not the person's voice.

WH-1000XM5 on the other hand are different and deserve 5 stars for that. I was able to have a normal conversation over the phone while walking outside in regular city traffic. I am not living in a big city like LA or NY but these headsets may perform well even there.
My wife told me that I sound like I am in the office.

The battery life is very good; I wear these for 3 weeks now listening on them 30 min every weekday and so far I didn't charge them at all. I like also the audio quality though so far I used these only for podcasts with limited music content.

Now for the not so good part. When I turn the ANC on, I can feel the noise cancelling kicking in, but I can still hear helicopters, airplanes, noisy traffic or even wind. I do believe that a larger ear cup with more foam could help with this issue. I heard that the ear cups are smaller than on WH-1000XM4 and frankly I would prefer these to be a bit larger.

Also the manual shows these as not being sweat proof and unfortunately after wearing these for a half an hour I do find sweat under the ear cups.

When I turn my head left or right it will increase the noise that comes from the outside. Not sure if it's related to the headband being more rigid or to the frame of my sunglasses keeping the cups at a certain distance from the ear.

The tactile control works good, is responsive, and provides feedback similar to the airpods, but I should read the manual to learn about all the features.

Overall I am pleased with the purchase and I would recommend this product.

PS: I didn't own the WH-1000XM4 but I listened to some audio samples in a review and WH-1000XM5 seems to do a better job on noise canceling than WH-1000XM4.
all_reviews.reviewTitle: Best noise canceling on phone calls

all_reviews.reviewText: Best noise canceling headphones hands down, but I took one star away for a couple of reasons. First, the headband adjustment doesn't adjust for my head size. My head is smaller than the smallest size allowed by the headband. This gets bothersome because I want the headphones to fit my head snug, and they tend to fall/slide right off my head if I'm bending down to get something while I have them on. Also, the ear covering part swivels forward and backwards in an odd way. At times, it gets bothersome getting the right direction to put on the headphones because of this. One possible solution to this would be to add something to the ear cover part that would help find the right ear or left ear so a user using the headphones in the dark can easily find the correct ear cover and direction to place the headphones over the head and onto the correct ear, or the correct direction. Otherwise, these headphones are worth their weight in gold. LOVE LOVE LOVE MY WH1000 XM5

clientResponses.response:
Hi BMCc,

We regret to hear you're not having the best experience with these headphones and you find them uncomfortable during use. This is definitely not the experience we'd like to hear from you. Our WH-1000XM5 has a slider that you can use to adjust the length of the headband to get the best fit and for comfortable listening experience.

Note: If you put on the headset without adjusting the slider, the headset may not be positioned properly on your head, resulting in discomfort or a weak noise canceling effect. 

Ref: https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000539062.html

When the Adaptive Sound Control on the “Sony | Headphones Connect” app is turned on, the wearer's actions, like walking or running, are detected. The noise canceling function is automatically adjusted and the Ambient Sound Mode may be activated according to these actions. To make the most of the noise canceling function, turn off the Adaptive Sound Control, and try to turn on the noise canceling function manually when you need it.

It's unfortunate to know that the unit had to be returned. We hope you will consider Sony again in a future purchase. 
Thank you for your feedback.

Regards,
Jess

positive_reviews.reviewText:
There are a lot of noise canceling headsets on the market but even popular brands are not offering any support for phone calls. They are mostly focused on the person that wear the headsets not the person on the other end of the line. I would not be wrong if I tell you that they are making the things worse for the other person, by amplifying the background noise not the person's voice.

WH-1000XM5 on the other hand are different and deserve 5 stars for that. I was able to have a normal conversation over the phone while walking outside in regular city traffic. I am not living in a big city like LA or NY but these headsets may perform well even there.
My wife told me that I sound like I am in the office.

The battery life is very good; I wear these for 3 weeks now listening on them 30 min every weekday and so far I didn't charge them at all. I like also the audio quality though so far I used these only for podcasts with limited music content.

Now for the not so good part. When I turn the ANC on, I can feel the noise cancelling kicking in, but I can still hear helicopters, airplanes, noisy traffic or even wind. I do believe that a larger ear cup with more foam could help with this issue. I heard that the ear cups are smaller than on WH-1000XM4 and frankly I would prefer these to be a bit larger.

Also the manual shows these as not being sweat proof and unfortunately after wearing these for a half an hour I do find sweat under the ear cups.

When I turn my head left or right it will increase the noise that comes from the outside. Not sure if it's related to the headband being more rigid or to the frame of my sunglasses keeping the cups at a certain distance from the ear.

The tactile control works good, is responsive, and provides feedback similar to the airpods, but I should read the manual to learn about all the features.

Overall I am pleased with the purchase and I would recommend this product.

PS: I didn't own the WH-1000XM4 but I listened to some audio samples in a review and WH-1000XM5 seems to do a better job on noise canceling than WH-1000XM4.
